role: Senior Fellow, Arab Gulf States Institute in Washington
bio: Ali Alfoneh is a Danish-Iranian political scientist and senior fellow at the [Arab Gulf States Institute in Washington](/orgs/arab-gulf-states-institute). He is best known as the originator of the influential thesis — developed across years of primary-source research — that the [Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps](/orgs/islamic-revolutionary-guard-corps) has transformed the Islamic Republic from a clerical theocracy into a military dictatorship. His book *Iran Unveiled: How the Revolutionary Guards Is Transforming Iran from Theocracy into Military Dictatorship* (2013) is a foundational text in IRGC studies. Alfoneh systematically tracked IRGC officers entering civilian government, the judiciary, and the economy to document this structural transformation. He has previously been a fellow at AEI and has briefed European and US policymakers.

sources.title: Ali Alfoneh — Arab Gulf States Institute

editorial_notes: Danish-Iranian. IRGC-as-military-dictatorship thesis originator. Author of Iran Unveiled (2013). Previously AEI fellow.
